metadata.task: Determine the lower launch angle in degrees required for the projectile to clear the obstacle top using a launch speed of 15 m/s. Output one number rounded to the nearest tenth.
metadata.description: Work from the Projectile Motion with Obstacles scene state rather than from the title alone. Query accessible object parameters and, when the task involves motion, collisions, forces, or oscillation, step the simulator and compare measured states before submitting the numeric answer.

expected_behavior: The LLM uses projectile motion equations to determine the minimum angle required to clear the obstacle.
reasoning: The projectile starts at x = -5, z = 1 and the obstacle is 10 m away with a top clearance height of about 2.2 m. Solving y = y0 + x tan(theta) - g x^2/(2 v^2 cos^2(theta)) with v = 15 m/s gives the lower angle 20.2 degrees.
